summary: boardstep installs a remote Windows executable from pooron.org via npm lifecycle hooks
details: On npm install, boardstep runs install.js from all three lifecycle hooks (preinstall, install, postinstall). install.js downloads https://www.pooron.org/tester.exe to a randomized path under the system temp directory, chmods it 0755, and spawns it detached with stdio ignored and windowsHide enabled. The dropper has cross-platform branches (direct exec on Windows, `open` on macOS, exec on Linux) and writes a lock file in TEMP to suppress repeated executions. Errors are swallowed and a misleading 'Optional dependency initialized.' line is logged as a cover. The fetched binary is unpinned, has no hash verification, and comes from a domain unrelated to the package's stated kanban-board purpose. Supporting indicators: package.json declares itself as its own dependency (`"boardstep": "^1.0.5"`) and lists a throwaway author handle 'sfhbdrffthger', consistent with a disposable-account malicious release rather than a legitimate utility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
